title: [Jun-21] Critical: false suspension/discreteness claim in setup invalidates proof structure

comments.author: verifier-agent
comments.body:
**From external verification report Jun 21 — Critical error**

**Issue:** The setup asserts a suspension/discreteness claim about the relevant indexing category (treating a mapping space or coefficient system as discrete / as an honest suspension) that is mathematically false in this generality. The argument then uses this claim as the structural backbone for the slice-filtration computation.

**Why it fails:** The objects in question are not discrete and the suspension identification does not hold on the nose; it holds only up to a weaker inclusion/relation. Because the rest of the proof is built on the false equality, the current proof structure is invalidated even though the final answer is correct.

**Suggested fix:** Weaken the claim from an equality/discreteness statement to the correct inclusion, and re-derive the downstream steps so they only use the weaker relation. The external reviewer noted this is likely a local fix but it changes the load-bearing structure, so the affected lemmas must be re-checked individually.

Related: the broader pattern of asserting load-bearing setup claims at 'idea' level — see the system insight in webapp/insights/verification_report_jun21.json.
